questions.question:
The five items: A, B, C, D, and E are pushed in a stack, one after another starting from A.
The stack is popped four times and each element is inserted in a queue.
The two elements are deleted from the queue and pushed back on the stack.
Now one item is popped from the stack. The popped item is:
Queues serve major role in:
A double ended queue can be used to implement _____
The operations that can be done in a circular queue is/are:
Given a singly linked list. Let Q be the pointer to a node x in the middle of the list. What is the worst-case time complexity to delete the node x from the list?
What is the complexity of inserting n elements into an empty linked list, if the linked list needs to be maintained in sorted order?
Insertion of an element at the middle of a singly linked list requires the modification of how many pointers?
Which type of linked list stores the address of the head node in the next pointer of the last node?
How many distinct BSTs can be constructed from 3 distinct keys?
What are the worst case and average case complexities of a binary search tree?
Suppose the numbers 7, 5, 1, 8, 3, 6, 0, 9, 4, 2 are inserted in that order into an initially empty binary search tree. What is the in-order traversal sequence of the resultant tree?
Given a hash table T with 25 slots that stores 2000 elements, the load factor for T is:

Consider a hash table of size seven, with starting index zero, and a hash function (x+3) mod 4. Assuming the hash table is initially empty, which of the following is the contents of the table when the sequence 1, 3, 8, 10 is inserted into the table using linear probing? Here “_” denotes an empty location in the table.
A graph having an edge from each vertex to every other vertex is called _____
The number of elements in the adjacency matrix of a graph having 7 vertices is _____

For the following code snippet, represent in drawing the result of the given instructions on the used STL.
Write a template C++ class to implement a Binary Search Tree (BST) class. Your implementation should include the Node class with its constructor and the BST class with its constructor.

subQuestions.label: i
subQuestions.prompt: Implement Insert(), which inserts a value into the BST.

subQuestions.label: iii
subQuestions.prompt: Implement Max(), which returns the maximum value in the tree.

questions.question: A marketing agency wants a program to manage clients and influencers. Each client requests N influencers to promote a campaign, and each influencer has an ID, name, and Instagram ID.

subQuestions.label: i
subQuestions.prompt: Represent the required data using suitable STL(s).
